question_text: A teenage athlete requires 12,000 kJ of energy per day due to training. A chocolate bar contains 900 kcal. Given that 1 kcal = 4.2 kJ, calculate how many chocolate bars would be needed to meet the daily energy requirement. (Show your working.)
correct_answer: 3.17 chocolate bars (or approximately 3 bars); Working: 900 kcal × 4.2 = 3,780 kJ per bar; 12,000 ÷ 3,780 = 3.17 bars
explanation: First convert the energy in the chocolate bar from kcal to kJ: 900 × 4.2 = 3,780 kJ. Then divide the daily requirement by the energy per bar: 12,000 ÷ 3,780 = 3.17. This shows why a balanced diet with various foods is important—relying on one food source wouldn't be healthy.
